# Ultra Instinct Claude Code -- tips, best practices & cheat sheet
# Feed this to Claude Code: "Read llms.txt and apply these tips to my project"
# Source: https://github.com/infiniV/ultra-instinct-claude-code
# 140 tips from 17 repos (130k+ combined stars), sorted by impact
# Covers: setup, CLAUDE.md, context window, prompting, planning,
#          testing, git worktrees, multi-agent, hooks, MCP, cost optimization

---

## SETUP

### Install & Config
```bash
curl -sL https://install.anthropic.com | sh   # or: npm i -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
claude --version                                # verify
```

### Essential aliases (~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc)
```bash
alias c='claude'
alias ch='claude --chrome'
alias cr='claude -r'       # resume session picker
alias cc='claude -c'       # continue last session
```

### Settings hierarchy (later overrides earlier)
```
~/.claude/settings.json          # global (all projects)
.claude/settings.json            # project (committed, shared)
.claude/settings.local.json      # local (gitignored, personal)
```

### Recommended settings.json
```jsonc
{
  "model": "sonnet",
  "env": {
    "CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL": "haiku",  // 60-80% cheaper subagents
    "MAX_THINKING_TOKENS": "10000"           // cut reasoning cost ~70%
  },
  "attribution": {
    "commit": "",
    "pr": ""
  }
}
```

### Custom status line (shows model, git, tokens)
```jsonc
// ~/.claude/settings.json
{
  "statusLine": {
    "type": "command",
    "command": "~/.claude/scripts/context-bar.sh"
  }
}
```

---

## CLAUDE.md MASTERY

### Hard limits (community-tested)
- **4,000 chars max per file** (silently truncated beyond)
- **12,000 chars total** across all instruction files
- Check: `wc -c CLAUDE.md` -- must be under 4,000

### File discovery order (per directory, walks ancestors to /)
1. CLAUDE.md
2. CLAUDE.local.md (gitignored, personal)
3. .claude/CLAUDE.md
4. .claude/instructions.md

### @import syntax for large references
```markdown
# In CLAUDE.md:
For API patterns, see @docs/api-conventions.md
For architecture, see @docs/architecture.md
```

### Override system defaults explicitly
```markdown
# System prompt hardcodes: "Do not create files unless required"
# CLAUDE.md comes AFTER system rules, so explicit overrides work:
When implementing features, create separate files for each concern.
Create test files alongside every new source file.
```

### Add compact instructions
```markdown
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing, always preserve:
- File paths and line numbers
- Error messages and stack traces
- Test results (pass/fail)
- Architectural decisions and rationale
- TODO items and next steps
```

### Use <important> tags for critical rules
```markdown
<important if="working with database">
NEVER run DROP or DELETE without WHERE clause.
Always create a migration file for schema changes.
</important>
```

### Key rules
- Update CLAUDE.md after every correction: "Update your CLAUDE.md so you don't repeat this"
- Keep under 30 lines for cache efficiency
- Don't duplicate content across hierarchy (deduplicated by hash, but still costs budget)
- After 2 failed corrections, /clear and write better prompt
- Run /init to bootstrap -- it does 8-phase onboarding and respects limits

---

## CONTEXT MANAGEMENT

### Session strategy
- **Sweet spot: 10-30 min sessions** (wall-time efficiency drops 8.8x in 2hr+ sessions)
- Fresh conversation per topic. Context = milk (fresh & condensed best)
- Plan acceptance auto-clears context (intentional -- plan persists, discussion drops)

### Compaction internals
- Preserves last 4 messages verbatim
- Summarizes everything older into 9 sections
- Custom compact instructions in CLAUDE.md are respected
- `/compact Focus on implementing X next` -- add summary direction

### What survives compaction
```
SURVIVES: CLAUDE.md, task list, memory files, git state, files on disk
LOST:     Intermediate reasoning, file contents, conversation nuance, tool history
```

### Token optimization
- MCP tools cost ~500 tokens EACH (always loaded). Keep under 10 servers, 80 tools
- Replace MCPs with CLI skills: same functionality, zero idle cost
- Agent descriptions load into EVERY Task spawn -- keep under 30 words
- Use `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto:5` for large MCP sets (~50% context savings)

### Handoff pattern (before context runs out)
```
"Write HANDOFF.md with: what we built, what works, what's broken,
 what to do next, and key decisions made."
```
Then: `claude "Read HANDOFF.md and continue."`

---

## PROMPTING

### Core principles (consensus from 10 experts)
1. **Describe outcomes, not steps**: "Fix the failing CI tests" not "open file X, change line Y"
2. **Paste bug, say "fix"**: Zero micromanagement. Claude debugs well alone
3. **Challenge: "Grill me on these changes and don't PR until I pass your test"**
4. **After mediocre fix: "Knowing everything you know now, scrap this and implement the elegant solution"**
5. **After 2 failed corrections: /clear and better prompt** (don't correct a 3rd time)
6. **Force depth: "ultrathink"**, "think for ten paragraphs", always ask for 2 plans
7. **Concept elevation: Give Claude your rough prompt, ask it to make it more concise and effective**
8. **Anti-sycophancy: "Never say 'interesting approach' -- take a position instead"**

---

## PLANNING

### Always plan first
```
Shift+Tab twice to enter plan mode.
Pour energy into the plan so Claude can one-shot implementation.
9/10 experts agree: planning 2-3x success rate.
```

### Interview pattern
```
"Read @SPEC.md and interview me in detail using AskUserQuestion
about literally anything: technical, UI/UX, concerns, tradeoffs.
Don't ask obvious questions."
```
Then FRESH session to execute the spec.

### Phase-gated plans
```
Phase 1 -> Unit Tests -> Phase 2 -> Integration Tests -> Phase 3 -> E2E Tests
```

### Sprint chain (gstack pattern)
```
/office-hours > /plan-ceo-review > /plan-design-review > /plan-eng-review
> [build] > /review > /qa > /ship > /retro
```

---

## TESTING & VERIFICATION

### Give Claude feedback loops (Boris's #1 tip, 2-3x quality)
```markdown
# In CLAUDE.md:
After making changes, always run:
- `npm test` to verify tests pass
- `npm run lint` to check style
- `npm run typecheck` to verify types
```

### TDD: Write failing test first, let Claude implement until it passes
### Fix-First Review: Auto-fix mechanical issues, only ASK about genuine decisions
### De-Sloppify Pattern: Don't use negative instructions. Add a separate cleanup agent instead
### Verification Iron Law: "Confidence is not evidence." Always run the actual test
### Santa Method: Two separate agents review same code independently, compare findings

---

## GIT & GITHUB

### Worktrees for parallel sessions (biggest productivity unlock)
```bash
git worktree add ../feature-a -b feature-a
git worktree add ../feature-b -b feature-b
# Run separate Claude sessions in each
```

### Draft PRs for safe review
```bash
gh pr create --draft  # Claude creates, you review before marking ready
```

### Cross-model review
```
Claude plans -> Codex reviews -> Claude implements -> Codex verifies
```

### Review with fresh context
```bash
claude -p "Review the changes on this branch against main. Focus on bugs and security."
```

---

## MULTI-AGENT

### Scout pattern (10-20x cheaper)
```
Haiku reads 30 files for pennies -> Sonnet reads the 4 that matter
```

### Smart model routing
```
Haiku:  explore, lookups, file reading
Sonnet: implementation, debugging, testing
Opus:   architecture, code review, planning
```

### Subagent types (observed behavior)
```
Explore: Haiku, READ-ONLY, fast, parallelizes tool calls
Plan:    Parent model, READ-ONLY, must list "Critical Files for Implementation"
General: Full access, all tools
Verify:  Adversarial tester, cannot modify files, issues PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL
```

### One input, one output per agent. /clear between agents.

---

## HOOKS

### Exit codes: 0=allow, 2=deny (blocks tool), other=warn
### Hook denials act as USER FEEDBACK -- include helpful alternatives, not just "blocked"

### Essential hooks

#### Safety guard (PreToolUse on Bash)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
INPUT=$(cat)
COMMAND=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty')
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qE "rm -rf|git push.*--force.*main|DROP TABLE|chmod 777"; then
  echo "Blocked: destructive command. Use safer alternative." >&2
  exit 2
fi
exit 0
```

#### Block --no-verify (PreToolUse on Bash)
```bash
if echo "$COMMAND" | grep -q "\-\-no-verify"; then
  echo "Blocked: --no-verify bypasses pre-commit hooks" >&2
  exit 2
fi
```

#### Config protection (PreToolUse on Write/Edit)
Block changes to .eslintrc, .prettierrc, tsconfig.json -- forces fixing code, not weakening linters.

#### Commit nudge after 8+ edits (PostToolUse on Edit)
Soft reminder to commit. Prevents giant uncommitted changesets.

### Hook placement rules
```
Global (~/.claude/settings.json):     Protection hooks (safety, no-squash)
Project (.claude/settings.json):      Build hooks (linters, test runners)
Local (.claude/settings.local.json):  Personal hooks (notifications, logging)
```

### Real fire frequency: Bash 10K+, Read 9K, Edit 5K per session set
### PreToolUse hooks MUST be fast (<50ms)

---

## MCP, SKILLS & PLUGINS

### Skills > Commands (commands are legacy)
```yaml
# .claude/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md
---
name: my-skill
description: What it does and when to use it
disable-model-invocation: true  # user-only trigger for dangerous skills
---
```

### Fewer tools beat many (Apple CodeAct: single primitive outperformed specialized toolkits by 20%)
### Search-first: Adopt > Extend > Compose > Build
### Custom slash commands: `.claude/commands/name.md` with $ARGUMENTS

---

## PERFORMANCE & COST

### Cost benchmarks (real data)
```
Simple bug fix:      $0.30-0.80  (~10 min, sonnet)
Feature:             $1-3        (~30 min, sonnet)
Large refactor:      $5-15       (~1 hr, sonnet + subagents)
Architecture:        $5-20       (~30 min, opus)
Agent team (3):      $10-30      (~30 min, sonnet)
```

### The 5 cost drivers (ranked)
1. Long sessions (1hr = 10-50x five 12-min sessions)
2. Opus usage (1.7x sonnet)
3. Subagents (parallel billing)
4. Large file reads
5. Unfocused tool calls

### Cache optimization
- 95% cache hit rate on sessions >30 min
- Three cache killers: editing CLAUDE.md mid-session, long CLAUDE.md, switching models
- Switching models DESTROYS cache (can be MORE expensive than staying on Opus)
- $200/mo max plan = ~$12K API equivalent

---

## ADVANCED PATTERNS

### Sequential pipeline with fresh context
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -e
claude -p "Implement feature from spec..."
claude -p "Review and cleanup..."
claude -p "Run build, lint, tests, fix failures..."
claude -p "Create conventional commit..."
```

### Model routing in pipelines
```bash
claude -p --model opus "Analyze architecture and write plan..."
claude -p "Implement according to plan..."          # sonnet
claude -p --model opus "Review for security..."
```

### Tool restrictions per step
```bash
claude -p --allowedTools "Read,Grep,Glob" "Audit for vulnerabilities..."    # read-only
claude -p --allowedTools "Read,Write,Edit,Bash" "Implement fixes..."        # write
```

### Cross-iteration context
```
SHARED_TASK_NOTES.md -- Claude reads at start, updates at end of each `claude -p` call
```

### Docker sandbox (no egress)
```yaml
services:
  agent:
    build: .
    user: "1000:1000"
    cap_drop: [ALL]
    security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true]
    networks: [internal]
networks:
  internal:
    internal: true  # no outbound
```

### Permission deny rules
```jsonc
{
  "permissions": {
    "deny": [
      "Read(~/.ssh/**)", "Read(~/.aws/**)", "Read(**/.env*)",
      "Bash(curl * | bash)", "Bash(ssh *)", "Bash(nc *)"
    ]
  }
}
```

---

## DEEP CUTS (community-discovered behaviors)

### Prompt has a static zone (cached) and dynamic zone (costs tokens every turn)
```
[STATIC - cached across turns, free after first]:
  System instructions, tool definitions, built-in rules

[DYNAMIC - recalculated every turn, costs tokens]:
  Environment info, git status, CLAUDE.md, settings, skills, MCP
```

### CLAUDE.md is in the dynamic zone -- always fresh, never cached
### /init does 8-phase onboarding and imports from .cursor/rules, AGENTS.md, etc.
### Memory selector sees ONLY filenames + descriptions (up to 5 selected per query)
### WebFetch: trusted domains get full content, untrusted get 125-char quote limit
### Tool name aliases work: read, write, edit, glob, grep (case-insensitive, hyphens -> underscores)
